pattern: Classic logarithmic search over a sorted array by repeatedly halving an inclusive index window.
hints: Because the array is sorted, you can discard half of the remaining range with each comparison.
Maintain a low and high index and compare the target against the middle element to decide which half to keep.
Compute mid as low + (high - low) // 2 to avoid overflow, return mid on a match, and return -1 once low passes high.

stress.description: sorted distinct array with a guaranteed-present target
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    vals = sorted(rng.sample(range(-9999, 10000), min(n, 19999)))
    target = vals[rng.randrange(len(vals))]
    return [vals, target]

stress.description: sorted distinct array with a likely-absent target
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    vals = sorted(rng.sample(range(-9999, 10000), min(n, 19999)))
    target = rng.randrange(-9999, 10000)
    return [vals, target]
